title: DAVID — The Tongue Rome Learned Its Alphabet From

notes: Row 6: AI-disclosure obligation planned — verify presence at Pre-Publish.

config.voice_suffix: mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @David-001: identical host face, charcoal sweater, Archive desk, brass lamp, same eye-line — seamless continuation of prior take, zero jump cut. CONTINUITY LOCK @Set-Archive-001: identical Archive interior — floor-to-ceiling manuscript shelves, long wooden worktable, open illuminated codex, cuneiform tablet, rolled papyrus, single brass desk lamp, same shelf blur depth, same eye-line to desk — seamless continuation, zero jump cut. STYLE LOCK @Style-Documentary-Prestige-001: documentary prestige grade — rich midtones, restrained contrast, natural skin texture, subtle film grain, trustworthy broadcast gravitas, no beauty-filter smoothing, no Instagram saturation. Host seated at Archive worktable under neutral 5000K ambient key, speaks calmly to camera; motivated desk practical visible on codex surface only. Camera: slow gentle push-in medium. LIGHTING LOCK @Set-Archive-001 (#243): neutral balanced 5000K ambient key on host — natural skin, blue channel intact; soft 5200K shelf bounce ≤25%; motivated desk practical on worktable/codex in desk quadrant only — no warm spill on face, sweater, or shelves; no yellow-green cast; no blue-starved shadows; no magenta ambient. GENERATION LOCK Archive-neutral (#243): neutral balanced 5000K key at generation — skin midtones R≈G≈B within 8%, blue channel intact (B≥40 mids, B/R≥0.35); wood burnt umber #3D2B1F–#5C4033; parchment cream #F5F0E6; shadow neutral grey #3A3530. Motivated desk practical in lower desk quadrant only — zero warm spill on host face, sweater, or shadows. Forbid yellow-green cast. Forbid blue-starved generation. Forbid magenta cast. Forbid global orange skin push. Motivated practical accent only — no unmotivated beauty dish, no fashion rim, lamp or window must be visible or implied. Slow motivated push-in or locked-off medium; handheld only for insert B-roll — never jitter on host. Lip-synced, delivers: "I am DAVID. Today, Etruscan — the language isolate of ancient Italy, the civilization Rome absorbed and rarely credited, the tongue that gave Latin speakers their alphabet." mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Finish on gesture peak (hand motion or lean), never hold dead stillness or dead frame. Opening shot, establish environment slowly.
shots.speech_text: I am DAVID. Today, Etruscan — the language isolate of ancient Italy, the civilization Rome absorbed and rarely credited, the tongue that gave Latin speakers their alphabet.

Lip-synced, delivers: "In 1964, construction work near the ancient port of Pyrgi — harbour of the Etruscan city of Caere — unearthed three gold tablets. Two were in Etruscan, one in Phoenician, commemorating the same dedication to a goddess. The Pyrgi Tablets became the Rosetta Stone of Etruscan: not a full translation, but enough parallel text to anchor key vocabulary and confirm the phonetic values the alphabet already suggested." mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Finish on gesture peak (hand motion or lean), never hold dead stillness or dead frame. Continue from previous shot — same camera direction, smooth motion carry.
shots.speech_text: In 1964, construction work near the ancient port of Pyrgi — harbour of the Etruscan city of Caere — unearthed three gold tablets. Two were in Etruscan, one in Phoenician, commemorating the same dedication to a goddess. The Pyrgi Tablets became the Rosetta Stone of Etruscan: not a full translation, but enough parallel text to anchor key vocabulary and confirm the phonetic values the alphabet already suggested.

shots.on_screen: ATTESTED alphabet · PARTIAL vocabulary · UNKNOWN relatives

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @David-001: identical host face, charcoal sweater, Archive desk, brass lamp, same eye-line — seamless continuation of prior take, zero jump cut. CONTINUITY LOCK @Set-Archive-001: identical Archive interior — floor-to-ceiling manuscript shelves, long wooden worktable, open illuminated codex, cuneiform tablet, rolled papyrus, single brass desk lamp, same shelf blur depth, same eye-line to desk — seamless continuation, zero jump cut. STYLE LOCK @Style-Documentary-Prestige-001: documentary prestige grade — rich midtones, restrained contrast, natural skin texture, subtle film grain, trustworthy broadcast gravitas, no beauty-filter smoothing, no Instagram saturation. Host settles back slightly, reflective scholarly mode — hands composed on desk, slight lean back. Camera: medium — slight pullback. LIGHTING LOCK @Set-Archive-001 (#243): neutral balanced 5000K ambient key on host — natural skin, blue channel intact; soft 5200K shelf bounce ≤25%; motivated desk practical on worktable/codex in desk quadrant only — no warm spill on face, sweater, or shelves; no yellow-green cast; no blue-starved shadows; no magenta ambient. GENERATION LOCK Archive-neutral (#243): neutral balanced 5000K key at generation — skin midtones R≈G≈B within 8%, blue channel intact (B≥40 mids, B/R≥0.35); wood burnt umber #3D2B1F–#5C4033; parchment cream #F5F0E6; shadow neutral grey #3A3530. Motivated desk practical in lower desk quadrant only — zero warm spill on host face, sweater, or shadows. Forbid yellow-green cast. Forbid blue-starved generation. Forbid magenta cast. Forbid global orange skin push. Motivated practical accent only — no unmotivated beauty dish, no fashion rim, lamp or window must be visible or implied. Slow motivated push-in or locked-off medium; handheld only for insert B-roll — never jitter on host. Lip-synced, delivers: "The question of Etruscan's relatives has not been settled. Proposed connections include Raetic, spoken in the Alps, and Lemnian, attested in a single archaic inscription from the Greek island of Lemnos — together forming what some scholars call the Tyrsenian hypothesis. The connection is plausible but not proven. What is not in doubt is that Etruscan civilization transmitted the Greek alphabet to Rome, and from Rome to every Western language you have ever learned to read." mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Finish on gesture peak (hand motion or lean), never hold dead stillness or dead frame. Continue from previous shot — same camera direction, smooth motion carry.
shots.speech_text: The question of Etruscan's relatives has not been settled. Proposed connections include Raetic, spoken in the Alps, and Lemnian, attested in a single archaic inscription from the Greek island of Lemnos — together forming what some scholars call the Tyrsenian hypothesis. The connection is plausible but not proven. What is not in doubt is that Etruscan civilization transmitted the Greek alphabet to Rome, and from Rome to every Western language you have ever learned to read.

provenance_card.title: DAVID · The Archive
